<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

1→--- 2→description: Audit an objective against current codebase and reconcile stale references 3→--- 4→ 5→# /erk:objective-reconcile 6→ 7→Audits an open objective against the current codebase state, identifies stale references, already-done work, and outdated prose, then proposes and applies updates with user confirmation. 8→ 9→## Usage 10→ 11→```bash 12→/erk:objective-reconcile <issue-number> 13→``` 14→ 15→--- 16→ 17→## Agent Instructions 18→ 19→### Phase 1: Validate Input and Fetch Objective Context 20→ 21→Parse `$ARGUMENTS` for the issue number. If no issue number provided: 22→ 23→``` 24→Error: Issue number required. 25→Usage: /erk:objective-reconcile <issue_number> 26→``` 27→ 28→Fetch the objective issue: 29→ 30→**Do NOT use `objective-fetch-context`** — it requires plan/branch/PR context and fails on master.
... [25 lines omitted] ...
46→Error: Issue #<number> is not an erk-objective issue (missing erk-objective label).

50→ 51→``` 52→Warning: Objective #<number> is CLOSED. Proceeding with audit anyway. 53→``` 54→ 55→### Phase 2: Extract Auditable References 56→ 57→Systematically extract concrete references from the objective body that can be verified against the codebase. Build a checklist of items to audit. 58→ 59→**From roadmap node descriptions** (parsed directly from the issue body markdown tables and YAML metadata blocks, not from a pre-parsed field): 60→ 61→- File paths (e.g., `src/erk/foo.py`, `tests/test_bar.py`) 62→- Command names (e.g., `erk inspect`, `/local:some-command`, `erk exec script-name`) 63→- Function/class/module names mentioned as implementation targets 64→- CLI subcommand references 65→ 66→**From Implementation Context section:** 67→ 68→- Key Files list entries 69→- Architecture descriptions referencing specific modules or patterns 70→- Technology or library references 71→ 72→**From Design Decisions section:** 73→ 74→- Pattern references (e.g., "uses the gateway ABC pattern") 75→- Technology choices that may have changed 76→ 77→**From pending node descriptions:** 78→ 79→- Descriptions of work that may already be done via other PRs
... [55 lines omitted] ...

57→def _update_plan_header(issue_body: str, **updates: Any) -> str: 58→ """Apply field updates to the plan-header block in an issue body. 59→ 60→ Extracts the existing block, merges updates, validates, re-renders, 61→ and replaces the block in the body. 62→ 63→ Args: 64→ issue_body: Current issue body containing plan-header block. 65→ **updates: Field name/value pairs to set in the block data. 66→ 67→ Returns: 68→ Updated issue body string. 69→ 70→ Raises: 71→ ValueError: If plan-header block not found or validation fails. 72→ """ 73→ block = find_metadata_block(issue_body, BlockKeys.PLAN_HEADER) 74→ if block is None: 75→ raise ValueError("plan-header block not found in issue body") 76→ updated_data = dict(block.data) 77→ updated_data.update(updates) 78→ schema = PlanHeaderSchema() 79→ schema.validate(updated_data) 80→ new_block = MetadataBlock(key=BlockKeys.PLAN_HEADER, data=updated_data) 81→ return replace_metadata_block_in_body( 82→ issue_body, BlockKeys.PLAN_HEADER, render_metadata_block(new_block) 83→ ) 84→ 85→ 86→def create_plan_header_block(
... [5 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Classify PR review feedback mechanically before LLM processing. 2→ 3→This command handles deterministic classification of PR review feedback: 4→- Data fetching (reviews, threads, discussion comments) 5→- Bot detection ([bot] suffix) 6→- Mechanical classification (APPROVED → informational, CHANGES_REQUESTED → actionable) 7→- Restructuring detection (renamed/moved files) 8→- Pre-existing issue candidate flagging 9→ 10→The LLM (pr-feedback-classifier skill) only handles: 11→- Action summaries 12→- Complexity estimation 13→- Ambiguous review classification 14→- Batch construction 15→ 16→Usage: 17→ erk exec classify-pr-feedback 18→ erk exec classify-pr-feedback --pr 123 19→ erk exec classify-pr-feedback --include-resolved 20→ 21→Output: 22→ JSON with mechanically classified feedback (intermediate format for LLM) 23→ 24→Exit Codes: 25→ 0: Success (or graceful error with JSON output) 26→ 1: Context not initialized 27→""" 28→ 29→from __future__ import annotations 30→

60→ @property 61→ def clipboard(self) -> Clipboard: 62→ """Get the clipboard interface for copy operations.""" 63→ return self._clipboard 64→ 65→ @property 66→ def browser(self) -> BrowserLauncher: 67→ """Get the browser launcher interface for opening URLs.""" 68→ return self._browser 69→ 70→ def close_pr(self, pr_number: int, pr_url: str) -> list[int]: 71→ """Close a PR using direct HTTP calls. 72→ 73→ Args: 74→ pr_number: The PR number to close 75→ pr_url: The PR URL (unused, kept for interface consistency) 76→ 77→ Returns: 78→ Empty list (no linked PRs to close) 79→ """ 80→ owner = self._location.repo_id.owner 81→ repo = self._location.repo_id.repo 82→ 83→ self._http_client.patch( 84→ f"repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{pr_number}", 85→ data={"state": "closed"}, 86→ ) 87→ 88→ return [] 89→

session.tool_result: 13:def _get_show_hidden_from_context(ctx: click.Context) -> bool: 22: return bool(getattr(config, "show_hidden_commands", False)) 26: return installation.load_config().show_hidden_commands 41: """Command that respects show_hidden_commands config for hidden options. 45: show_hidden_commands is enabled in config. 50: show_hidden = _get_show_hidden_from_context(ctx) 59: if show_hidden: 80:def script_option(fn: F) -> F: 84: The --script flag is hidden by default but visible when show_hidden_commands=True.

80→def script_option(fn: F) -> F: 81→ """Decorator that adds --script option with proper settings. 82→ 83→ Must be applied to a function decorated with @click.command(cls=CommandWithHiddenOptions). 84→ The --script flag is hidden by default but visible when show_hidden_commands=True. 85→ 86→ Example: 87→ @click.command("up", cls=CommandWithHiddenOptions) 88→ @script_option 89→ def up_cmd(ctx: ErkContext, script: bool) -> None: 90→ ... 91→ """ 92→ return click.option( 93→ "--script", 94→ is_flag=True, 95→ hidden=True, 96→ help="Output shell script for integration. NOT a dry run.", 97→ )(fn) 98→
